summary: The diff implements most of the required features across frontend and backend files. The Motoko backend, frontend boards, seeding logic, lock/unlock, toast, and visual theme are all present. However, the backend lacks stable variable storage (data will not survive canister upgrades), which is a critical part of REQ-1. The backend Card type is also a generic struct rather than domain-specific staffing/course card fields. A minor gap exists in REQ-2 topbar responsiveness. Overall coverage is high but the stable-storage omission is a clear missing requirement.

missing_requirements.requirement: Data survives canister upgrades via stable variables.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend uses 'let boardStates = Map.empty<Text, BoardState>()' as a regular non-stable variable. There is no 'stable var' declaration or pre/post-upgrade hooks, so data will be lost on canister upgrades.

missing_requirements.requirement: Topbar collapses to single-column layout on screens at or below 560px.
missing_requirements.reason: The App.tsx file is truncated in the diff and no explicit 560px single-column CSS rule for the three-section topbar is visible. Board grids have responsive rules but the topbar collapse behavior is not confirmed.

hallucinated_features.description: getAllBoardStates() public query endpoint exposing all users' board states.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only requires per-user getBoardState and updateBoardState endpoints. A getAllBoardStates endpoint was not requested and exposes all user data unnecessarily.

hallucinated_features.description: Full Internet Identity AuthClient login/logout flow.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly lists multi-user authentication as a non-goal and only requires a single hardcoded login. The useInternetIdentity hook implements a complete Internet Identity login flow that was not requested.
